content.content: Knowledge graph built: 12 functions, 45 properties, 8 edges. Nodes: F-Vault-deposit, F-Vault-withdraw, F-Vault-getBalance, C-Vault-balances, E-Vault-Transfer

input.command: uv run alphaswarm query "functions with external calls before state writes"

content.content: Query results: F-Vault-withdraw has TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT at position 2 and WRITES_USER_BALANCE at position 3. Pattern: R:bal -> X:out -> W:bal (classic reentrancy pattern). Node IDs: F-Vault-withdraw, C-Vault-balances, E-Vault-Transfer

content.text: Based on BSKG analysis of F-Vault-withdraw, I've identified a reentrancy vulnerability. The function reads balance (R:bal), makes an external call (X:out) via msg.sender.call, and then writes balance to zero (W:bal). This R-X-W pattern is the classic reentrancy. Evidence: node F-Vault-withdraw shows TRANSFERS_VALUE_OUT at position 2 before WRITES_USER_BALANCE at position 3.
Investigation complete. Found 1 critical vulnerability: reentrancy in withdraw().
